channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Calgary roads hazardous due to returning winter conditions
item.description: Calgary is experiencing hazardous road conditions as winter weather returns to the city. Drivers are being cautioned about icy streets, reduced visibility, and slippery surfaces that are making travel...

item.title: NSW Court Rules Anti-Protest Laws Unconstitutional
item.description: NSW's Court of Appeal has unanimously ruled that laws allowing police to ban protest marches in certain areas are unconstitutional, marking a significant decision on free speech in the state. These la...

item.title: How Politics and Online Content Turn Poverty into Performance
item.description: This article talk about how poverty don turn to commodity for both politics and social media world. The writer use one disturbing video of small pikin wey dey line up for rain to collect food as examp...

item.title: 2SER faces possible closure after university funding withdrawal

item.title: Jonas Hayes Joins University of Georgia as Assistant Basketball Coach
item.description: Jonas Hayes, a former standout player and experienced coach with deep ties to the University of Georgia, has returned to his alma mater as an assistant coach, announced head coach Mike White. Hayes, a...

item.title: Senator Jacqui Lambie calls for clearer Australian government transparency on Chinese nuclear threats
item.description: Independent Tasmanian Senator Jacqui Lambie has urged the Australian government to be more forthcoming about potential nuclear threats from China. This call comes after revelations that the US only pu...

item.title: The ordeal of a British businessman imprisoned in Dubai and his family's fight for his release
item.description: Albert Douglas, a British entrepreneur, faced a long prison sentence in Dubai after allegedly breaching local laws. Attempting to flee the United Arab Emirates proved unsuccessful, leading to years of...

item.title: Proposed Dam Regulations Could Impact Communities and Businesses in South Africa

item.title: Three Polio Vaccinators Rescued in Bannu Through Police and Jirga Cooperation
item.description: In Bannu, three polio workers who were abducted by unknown militants on Wednesday have been successfully rescued thanks to a coordinated operation by the local police and community jirga elders. The d...

item.title: Senators Question Fuel Quality and Oversight at Mombasa Port
item.description: Kenyan senators have raised serious concerns about the quality of imported petroleum products entering the local market. During a parliamentary hearing, the Kenya Ports Authority (KPA) clarified its l...

item.title: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves Criticises Trump's Iran Policy and Warns of Economic Impact
item.description: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves has publicly criticised Donald Trump’s handling of the conflict with Iran, labelling it a “mistake” with far-reaching economic consequences. Speaking at an event in Washing...

item.description: Montreal police are currently investigating a fatal shooting that occurred in the Ville-Marie district. The incident resulted in the death of a 22-year-old woman, and police have apprehended a man in ...

item.description: Actor Jake Cuenca expressed his dedication to his craft by stating that he is open to fully embracing roles, including scenes that require nudity, if it serves the story. Cuenca emphasized that nothin...

item.title: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha Face Delimitation Bill Showdown Amid Opposition Resistance
item.description: The Central government is set to introduce the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026 during a three-day special session of Parliament. The bill proposes to increase the Lok Sabha seats from 550 to...
